page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz with an arm64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 11,400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In multi-core and memory-intensive workloads, the r8gd.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance. Multi-core stress-ng benchmarks and Geekbench multi-core scores place this instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 21,309.29 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 411.52 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 46,718.3 and Redis SET operations achieving 361.71 million operations per second. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1,653.0.

The integration of custom AWS Graviton silicon and local NVMe SSD storage provides high resource density and cost efficiency for large-scale deployments. By avoiding hyperthreading and utilizing 192 dedicated physical cores, the instance minimizes resource contention. This profile is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput key-value stores like Redis, large-scale software compilation, and data-intensive web serving. It is less optimal for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or specialized GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.48xlarge is a memory-optimized, arm64-based instance designed for high-throughput, data-intensive workloads. Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors, it delivers 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of RAM and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the r8gd.48xlarge achieves top-tier multi-core and memory performance, notably in Redis SET operations and PassMark database tests, while single-core performance remains average. The combination of high memory capacity, dedicated physical cores, and massive local storage provides excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 ARM64 physical cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.48xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 192 physical cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 platform combining massive memory capacity and local NVMe storage for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 processors.
1536.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio.
11,400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and data-intensive web serving.
